volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 2.2.366b - Section 2.5.425)

sections.label: Section 2.2.366b
sections.translation: Do these things seem to accord with a wedding ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.367
sections.translation: I think not , Davus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.367b
sections.translation: Think , do you say ? You don ’ t view it rightly ; the thing is certain . Besides , coming away from there I saw the servant-boy of Chremes carrying some vegetables and little fishes , an obol ’ s worth , [*] for the old man ’ s dinner .

sections.label: Section 2.2.370
sections.original: Liberatus sum hodie Dave tua opera .
sections.translation: This day , Davus , have I been delivered by your means .

sections.label: Section 2.2.370b
sections.translation: And yet not at all .

sections.label: Section 2.2.371
sections.original: Quid ita ? Nempe huic prorsus illam non dat .
sections.translation: Why so ? Surely he will not give her to him , after all this . ( Pointing to PAMPHILUS .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.371b
sections.translation: You silly fellow ! as though it were a necessary consequence that if he doesn ’ t give her to him you should marry her : unless , indeed , you look about you ; unless you entreat and make court to the old man ’ s friends .

sections.label: Section 2.2.373b
sections.translation: You advise well . I ’ ll go ; although , upon my faith , this hope has often eluded me already . Farewell !

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.original: Quid igitur sibi vult pater ? cur simulat ?
sections.translation: What then does my father mean ? Why does he thus make pretense ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.375b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . If now he were angry with you , because Chremes will not give you a wife , he would seem to himself to be unjust , and that not without reason , before he has ascertained your feelings as to the marriage , how they are disposed . But if you refuse to marry her , in that case he will transfer the blame to you ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.translation: then such disturbances will arise .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380b
sections.translation: I will submit to any thing from him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.380c
sections.translation: He is your father , Pamphilus . It is a difficult matter . Besides , this woman is defenseless . No sooner said than done ; he will find some pretext for driving her away from the city .

sections.label: Section 2.3.382b
sections.translation: Driving her away ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.382c
sections.translation: Aye , and quickly too .

sections.label: Section 2.3.383
sections.translation: Tell me then , Davus , what am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.383b
sections.translation: Say that you will marry her .

sections.label: Section 2.3.383c
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 2.3.383d
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.384
sections.translation: What , am I to say so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.384b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.384c
sections.translation: Never will I do it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.384d
sections.translation: Don ’ t say so .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.translation: Don ’ t attempt to persuade me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385b
sections.original: Ex ea re quid fiat vide .
sections.translation: Consider what will be the result of it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.386
sections.original: Ut ab illa excludar huc concludar .
sections.translation: That I shall be deprived of the one , and fixed with the other .

sections.label: Section 2.3.386b
sections.translation: Not so . In fact , I think it will be thus : Your father will say : I wish you to marry a wife to-day . You reply : I ’ ll marry her . Tell me , how can he raise a quarrel with you ? Thus you will cause all the plans

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: Quae nunc sunt certa ei consilia incerta ut sient ,
sections.translation: which are now arranged by him to be disarranged , without any danger ; for this is not to be doubted , that Chremes will not give you his daughter . Therefore do not hesitate in those measures which you are taking , on this account , lest he should change his sentiments . Tell your father that you consent ; so that although he may desire it , he may not be able to be angry at you with reason .

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: Nam quod tu speres propulsabo facile . Uxorem his moribus
sections.translation: For that which you rely on , I will easily refute ; No one , you think , will give a wife to a person of these habits . But he will find a beggar for you , rather than allow you to be corrupted by a mistress . If , however , he shall believe that you bear it with a contented mind , you will render him indifferent ; at his leisure he will look out for another wife for you ; in the mean time something lucky may turn up .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399
sections.translation: Do you think so ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.399b
sections.translation: It really is not a matter of doubt .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399c
sections.translation: Consider to what you are persuading me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399d
sections.translation: Nay , but do be quiet .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: Dicam . Puerum autem ne resciscat mihi esse ex illa cautio est :
sections.translation: Well , I ’ ll say it ; but , that he mayn ’ t come to know that she has had a child by me , is a thing to be guarded against ; for I have promised to bring it up .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401b
sections.translation: Oh , piece of effrontery .

sections.label: Section 2.3.401c
sections.translation: She entreated me that I would give her this pledge , by which she might be sure she should not be deserted .

sections.label: Section 2.3.403
sections.original: Curabitur . Sed pater adest . Cave te esse tristem sentiat .
sections.translation: It shall be attended to ; but your father ’ s coming . Take care that he doesn ’ t perceive that you are out of spirits .

sections.label: Section 2.4.404
sections.original: Reviso quid agant aut quid captent consili .
sections.translation: I ’ ve come back to see what they are about , or what scheme they are hatching .

sections.label: Section 2.4.405
sections.original: Hic nunc non dubitat quin te ducturum neges .
sections.translation: He has no doubt at present but that you ’ ll refuse to marry . Having considered his course , he ’ s come from a retired spot somewhere or other ; he hopes that he has framed a speech by which to disconcert you ; do you take care , then , to be yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.409
sections.translation: If I am only able , Davus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.409b
sections.translation: Trust me for that , Pamphilus , I tell you ; your father will never this day exchange a single word with you , if you say that you will marry .

sections.label: Section 2.5.412
sections.original: Herus me relictis rebus iussit Pamphilum
sections.translation: My master has ordered me , leaving my business , to keep an eye on Pamphilus today , what he is doing with regard to the marriage . I was to learn it ; for that reason , I have now followed him [*] ( pointing to SIMO .) as he came hither .

sections.label: Section 2.5.415
sections.original: Ipsum adeo praesto video cum Davo : hoc agam .
sections.translation: Himself , as well , I see standing with Davus close at hand ; I ’ ll note this .

sections.label: Section 2.5.416
sections.translation: I see that both of them are here .

sections.label: Section 2.5.416b
sections.translation: Now then , be on your guard .

sections.label: Section 2.5.416c
sections.translation: Pamphilus !

sections.label: Section 2.5.417
sections.original: Quasi de inproviso respice ad eum .
sections.translation: Look round at him as though taken unawares .

sections.label: Section 2.5.417b
sections.translation: What , my father !

sections.label: Section 2.5.418
sections.translation: Capital !

sections.label: Section 2.5.418b
sections.original: Hodie uxorem ducas ut dixi volo .
sections.translation: I wish you to marry a wife to-day , as I was saying .

sections.label: Section 2.5.419
sections.original: Nunc nostrae timeo parti quid hic respondeat .
sections.translation: Now I ’ m in dread for our side , as to what he will answer .

sections.label: Section 2.5.420
sections.original: Neque istic neque alibi tibi erit usquam in me mora .
sections.translation: Neither in that nor in any thing else shall you ever find any hesitation in me .

sections.label: Section 2.5.420b
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 2.5.421
sections.translation: He is struck dumb .

sections.label: Section 2.5.421b
sections.translation: What a speech !

sections.label: Section 2.5.421c
sections.translation: You act as becomes you , when that which I ask I obtain with a good grace .

sections.label: Section 2.5.423
sections.translation: Am I right ?

sections.label: Section 2.5.423b
sections.translation: My master , so far as I learn , has missed his wife .

sections.label: Section 2.5.424
sections.original: I nunc iam intro ; ne in mora cum opus sit sies .
sections.translation: Now , then , go in-doors , that you mayn ’ t be causing delay when you are wanted .

sections.label: Section 2.5.425
sections.translation: I ’ ll go .
